top_findings.summary: The Background Checker skill requests local system permissions (Glob, Grep, Bash) that are excessive and unnecessary for its stated purpose of integrating with external background check services and analyzing publicly available information. While WebFetch is appropriate for accessing external databases, the ability to search local filesystems (Glob), scan file contents (Grep), and execute arbitrary system commands (Bash) is unrelated to verifying credentials or analyzing social media through third-party services. This creates potential for accessing sensitive local data beyond what's required for background checks.
